mission.title: cert-manager: Support for OpenShift `route` object
mission.description: Support for OpenShift `route` object. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Feature request: Support for the `route` object of OpenShift.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for OpenShift `route` object
steps.description:
adds injection to openshift routes
adds ability to present certificates as keystores and javastores

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close that issue when PR gets merged)*: fixes #1064
The following is missing:
1. registering the controllers to the cainjectior manager, need help with that
2. disabling the route injection controller if routes are not available need .1 first
3. verify where I added some needed constants (controller/util.go)
4.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for OpenShift `route` object" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
adds injection to openshift routes
adds ability to present certificates as keystores and javastores

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close that issue when PR gets merged)*: fixes #1064
The following is missing:
1. registering the controllers to the cainjectior manager, need help with that
2.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
